instructions: Podspine turns audiobook files into per-chapter podcast RSS feeds (Rust workspace, shells out to ffmpeg/ffprobe). Guard the invariants that keep feeds correct and the server safe; don't nitpick formatting or lint that cargo fmt / clippy -D warnings already enforce, and don't restate what tests already cover.

rules.rule: Feed items must have strictly sequential pubDates (oldest = chapter 1), plus itunes:episode and itunes:duration, and an enclosure length read from the real output file (fs::metadata().len()) — NEVER prorated from a bitrate. The self-check must reject a feed that violates these. Flag any regression to feed ordering, the itunes tags, the real-size enclosure length, or guid stability (blake3(book.id:idx:source_mtime)).

ffmpeg/ffprobe are invoked out-of-process with an ARGUMENT VECTOR, never a shell string — chapter titles, filenames, and tags are untrusted. Flag any construction of an ffmpeg/ffprobe command as a shell string, or interpolation of embedded metadata into a command line.

Book/episode ids are opaque index keys: validate slugs against the [a-z0-9-] allow-list, resolve to a file path server-side, and canonicalize the resolved path and assert it stays under the data dir (404 on reject, not 403). Flag any path built from request input via format!/join without the allow-list + canonicalize-prefix guard.
Podspine ships NO DRM circumvention. DRM inputs (.aax/.aaxc/.aa/.odm) are skipped with a logged notice. Flag any code that attempts to decrypt or work around DRM.

Many tests synthesize fixtures with ffmpeg and return early with a skip notice when ffmpeg or a specific encoder (aac/libmp3lame/flac/libopus) is unavailable. Do NOT flag that gated skip-return as dead code, an early return bug, or a silently-passing test.
